p.font: Welcome to the Odoo Custom Industrial Equipment package.

template.p: This end-to-end solution is engineered to streamline complex lifecycles—from Engineer-to-Order (ETO) and turnkey projects to high-stakes production. It provides seamless oversight across:

li.p: CRM & Relationship Management
Prototyping & Iteration
Client Approval Workflows
Project Management & Production Cycles
Post-Project Maintenance

template.p: You can access every installed App in your Odoo database on your main dashboard.

ul.li: CRM: Manage your customer relationships and pipelines.
Project: Build your project foundation for prototyping and reuse it for all subsequent stages.
Manufacturing & PLM: Handle production workflows and BOM versioning.
Purchase: Track supplier orders and manage your entire procurement history.
Inventory: Maintain stock accuracy and ensure you never dip below minimum levels.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 px-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: Odoo is entirely integrated into an App. Download it to manage your entire business from your phone.

template.p: For this industry, we’re using a custom conveyor system as our example. This includes systems designed 100% from scratch to meet specific client needs, as well as those that reuse standard parts to keep design costs down.
By uploading this module's demo data, your database has been filled with a few contacts.
To get a rapid understanding of the processes this Odoo package offers, you can execute the following flows and configure the settings to suit your business.
Of course, this is just an overview of the features included in this package. Feel free to add new apps, delete/modify demo data, and test everything around!

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-warning pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: You didn't import demo data? You can still do it. Go to Apps > Industries > Upgrade your Custom Industrial Equipment package and select the relevant checkbox

template.p: Efficient catalog management is the foundation of a seamless production workflow. To ensure data integrity and operational agility, our products are classified into four distinct categories, each with specific management protocols:

li.p: Definition: All components and materials sourced directly from external vendors.
Protocol: Maintain up-to-date vendor assignments and purchase pricing to ensure procurement accuracy.
Definition: In-house manufactured items used as sub-assemblies for larger builds rather than being sold individually.
Protocol: Develop detailed Bills of Materials (BOMs) that explicitly link components to their respective production stages and operations.
Definition: Sale-ready goods ranging from standardized items (fixed BOMs) to fully customized solutions.

Protocol: For new clients or projects, create a unique product entry. Map these to a Master BOM Catalog to pull in reusable templates; this allows for distinct database tracking while leveraging standardized production logic.
Definition: All billable, non-tangible offerings provided to the client.
Scope: Includes design, consulting, installation, and long-term maintenance.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Use Unique Serial Numbers to distinguish between identical products. This is essential for accurate maintenance, ensuring that every service action is recorded against the specific unit rather than the generic model.

p.span: To manage your production workflow, go to the Manufacturing application. From there, you can create your work centers, operations, and bills of materials.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3

ul.li: Go to Settings to create and configure your work centers, which are the locations where your production operations happen. You can also adjust their specific parameters to fit your needs.

template.p: To create a new Bill of Materials from scratch, you'll need to know that there are two distinct types: Production BOMs (for in-house production) and Subcontracting BOMs (for external production). Each type requires different settings. If you want to learn more about subcontracting, feel free to explore our documentation. Odoo manages complex subcontracting workflows, including component fulfillment, dropshipping, real-time stock tracking, and much more.

li.p: Navigate to the Bill of Material section and create a new BOM.

ol.li: For Standard Products: Link the BOM directly to the specific product you are creating. For Generic reusable BOMs, link them to a non-salable "Generic Product" and use a unique BOM Reference name to distinguish it from others in the catalog.

li.p: Add all the components needed for production and adjust their quantities.
Adjust the Manufacturing Lead Time (the time needed for production) and the Days to Prepare Manufacturing (the time needed to replenish or produce all components).
Create the operations, link them to their respective work centers, and specify the required time for each.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Even if your products are highly customized with unique Bills of Materials (BOMs), you can utilize a generic BOM as a template. By adapting the template directly within the specific Manufacturing Order, you avoid the need to build every structure from scratch

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3

li.p: By-products: Manage leftovers from production and track them as valuable inventory.
Work Center Capacity: Optimize your production schedule by exploring the capacity of your work centers.
Use the batch size setting: If you always produce in batches of a specific quantity, use the Batch Size setting in the Miscellaneous tab.
MTO (Make-to-Order) Route: Use this to automatically trigger a Manufacturing Order whenever a Sales Order is confirmed

template.p: Customers can request a quote directly from your website's contact form. You can then follow up with them in the CRM app to clarify their needs using the chatter feature.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: You can also set up an email alias that automatically generates a new opportunity directly within your CRM pipeline

template.p: Once their requirements are clear, you have two possible workflows:

p.b: Either you create a Quotation from the opportunity and wait for a confirmation.

li.p: Either create a project : From the CRM, create a new project by clicking the ⚙️ icon and selecting a template. This project will allow you to manage all the pre-sales steps, such as:
Design validation
Pattern creation
Shared information about important documents
etc...

template.p: This project structure offers full flexibility to adapt to your unique workflow.

For example: A client requests a specialized scanner capable of multilingual package recognition. Within a single project, you can centralize product specifications, vendor quotes, and BOM designs. You can then generate an initial quotation for a Proof of Concept (PoC) directly from the project. Once confirmed, you can continue iterating within the same project—creating updated quotations and Manufacturing Orders by building on previous data and new insights.
The Project app centralizes all the necessary information, ensuring a smooth and perfectly managed follow-up process.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Create project templates within the Project app to easily reuse for similar projects.

li.div: Centralized Hub: Use the top menu to navigate effortlessly between all elements linked to your project.
Targeted Delegation: Create and assign tasks directly to the appropriate team members to ensure accountability.
Flexible Timesheets: Log time on individual tasks to monitor project profitability or accurately bill clients for hours worked.
Sales & Purchase Orders: Generate unlimited Sales Orders to account for scope changes, and manage all project-specific Purchase Orders in one place.
Invoice Oversight: Track all related outgoing and incoming invoices directly within the project interface.
Financial Forecasting & Actuals: Compare estimated costs against real costs directly from the project overview to maintain control over your budget.
Organized File Sharing: Create custom folders (such as "Internal" or "Client-Facing") via the Documents button.
Client Collaboration: Share specific folders with your clients, providing them with a secure, central location to upload and access vital project files.
BOM Integration: If your project requires multiple Bills of Materials (BOMs), create them directly using the dedicated BOM button.
Direct Manufacturing: Launch Manufacturing Orders (MOs) from the Manufacturing tab. Once confirmed, these will flow into your standard manufacturing workflow. (see next section)

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.div: Want to compare your forecasted margins against actual costs?

li.div: Set Forecasts: Manually input your expected margin on each Sales Order line.
Monitor Progress: Visit the Overview Tab to see a side-by-side comparison of your sales, expected costs, and real costs (derived from invoices and completed Manufacturing Orders).

div.div: For a deeper dive into financial tracking, explore our documentation on Analytic Accounting.

template.p: Manufacturing Orders (MOs) can be generated from an existing Bill of Materials (BOM) or built from scratch. Regardless of the source, you maintain full control and can manually adjust the MO at any time.
If your final product requires sub-assemblies or semi-finished goods, you have three primary workflows to choose from:

li.p: Manual Creation: Generate secondary MOs for sub-assemblies manually as the need arises.
Automated MTO (Make to Order): Enable the MTO route on semi-finished products. This automatically triggers a linked secondary MO the moment the main manufacturing order is confirmed.

Make to Stock (Reordering Rules): Set a minimum stock level for your sub-assemblies. The system will automatically trigger a new MO once your inventory drops below this threshold, ensuring you always have enough stock on hand for future production.

template.p: Before confirming an MO, you can review and refine both the component list and the specific work operations:

li.p: Detailed Work Orders: For every operation, you can define precise timings, step-by-step instructions, and mandatory Quality Checks.
Work Assignments: Directly assign tasks to the appropriate personnel to streamline the production flow.

Miscellaneous Notes & Integration: Use the HTML field (note) in the Miscellaneous tab for advanced instructions. You can embed links to internal knowledge articles, 3D plans, or external software. Operators can access these links directly from the Shopfloor App during production.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.div: The system scales with your complexity. Explore our documentation to learn more about:

li.div: By-Products: Manage secondary outputs during the manufacturing process.
Work Center Capacity: Balance workloads and prevent bottlenecks based on resource availability.

div.div: Every manufacturing environment is unique. We recommend reviewing our full documentation to tailor these features to your specific production needs.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: If your business needs to easily toggle quality levels for individual components, check out this module for seamless upgrades and downgrades

template.p: Once production orders are confirmed, they need to be planned. The scheduled date on the Manufacturing Order (MO) is just an initial estimate and doesn't consider your actual capacity.

To plan your orders, go to the manufacturing app, select them, and click "Plan." The system will then generate a detailed schedule that accounts for the capacity of your work centers. You can see a clear overview of your schedule on the planning by work centers screen.
While you can manually move an operation with a simple drag and drop, keep in mind this change is isolated to that specific work order and will not re-plan the others.

Once production is planned, workers can execute and monitor their tasks using the Shop Floor App. This app can be installed as a standalone application. It's designed to be used by multiple employees on a single device, with each employee registering their work individually.
To get started:

li.p: Open the app, select the work centers and log in as an employee.
If a Manufacturing Order is planned and all necessary components are available, it will appear on your screen.
Select the work order to begin, follow the instructions, and mark it as done when complete.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Pro-Tip: You can print and use barcode commands with a scanner to automate and streamline your workflow.

template.p: The Shopfloor App serves as a direct bridge between production and quality management, allowing workers to maintain high standards and suggest improvements in real time.
You can handle two types of quality interventions directly from the shopfloor:

li.p: Proactive Quality Checks: These are predefined checks (created in the Quality App) that trigger automatically at specific stages of production.
Reactive Quality Alerts: If a worker identifies an issue on the fly, they can trigger a Quality Alert. This creates a dedicated task in the Quality App for the quality team to investigate.

template.p: The Shopfloor App empowers workers to contribute to process optimization through the PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) integration:

li.p: Propose BOM Updates: If a worker discovers a more efficient method or a necessary adjustment, they can propose adding a new step or component.
Engineering Review: This proposal automatically generates an ECO in the PLM app. Engineers can then review, test, and approve the change before it is officially applied to the Bill of Materials.

template.p: The PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) App is your dedicated environment for managing the evolution of your products. It allows you to review and iterate on every Bill of Materials (BOM) update, ensuring that all modifications are tracked and visible within a specialized application.

li.div: Version Control: Maintain a clear history of every BOM iteration.
Centralized Updates: Manage all changes and approvals within a single, dedicated interface.

li.p: Audit Trail: Keep every modification visible to ensure full transparency throughout the product's development cycle.

template.p: The Inventory app allows you to manage all of your stock operations, including inputs, outputs, and internal movements. When you validate a purchase or sales order, a corresponding receipt or delivery is automatically created.

Purchase orders can be created automatically using reordering rules. However, if you don't use reordering rules or want to manually review future needs, you can use the Replenishment view in the Inventory app.

The Replenishment view gives you a clear overview of all current and future needs (you can adjust the Horizon settings to see what's coming up). From this screen, you can directly create purchase orders, subcontracting orders, or manufacturing orders.
template.div: Once you've created a purchase order, validate it in the Purchase app. This powerful app allows you to group orders, compare different supplier offers, track receipt accuracy, and more.
template.p: When you're ready to manage a transfer:

li.p: Open the transfer.
Adjust the quantity and add lot numbers as needed.
Change the destination location if necessary.
If needed, you can pack the products together to generate a single label for the entire package.

template.p: Note: If you validate a transfer for a partial quantity, the system will automatically create a backorder for the rest of the items.
Use barcode scanners to validate stock movements, identify pallet positions, and perform fast, accurate stock operations. Greatly improves warehouse efficiency and reduces human error.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3

ul.li: You can customize the settings for each operation type within the barcode application to control what is permitted during the process. This allows you to define specific rules for operations like picking, ensuring the barcode functionality aligns with your business workflow.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3

ul.li: The barcode app can be installed as a standalone application on devices. This means people can use it without having access to the rest of the database.
You can print barcode commands to streamline your operations with quick scanning actions.

template.p: Once delivery is confirmed, you can schedule the on-site installation using the Planning app. This tool allows you to create shifts and allocate specific resources, including both personnel and equipment.

Each shift includes a customizable Worksheet to capture all necessary data for the final report. You can even include photos before having the client sign off. If a future maintenance visit is required, you can pre-schedule the next shift now and assign the specific resources at a later date.

During installation or maintenance, you may need to replace broken or incompatible parts. To maintain Odoo’s robust component traceability after a product is installed on-site, use the Repair app. By selecting the specific product, you can accurately add or remove components, ensuring your records reflect the true "as-built" configuration.

div.class: o_editor_banner user-select-none o-contenteditable-false lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-success pb-0 pt-3
o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true w-100 px-3
div.p: Seamless Integration: Create a repair order directly from a Helpdesk task to streamline your after-sales service.
Easy Billing: Generate a Sales Order directly from the repair order to invoice your client for parts and labor.

template.p: You can easily invoice clients directly from a Sales Order. Each order allows you to set specific payment terms and conditions tailored to that client or project.

When you're ready, you have the flexibility to create an invoice for either a full or a partial payment, such as a down payment. All invoices generated are tracked and can be managed through the Invoices app.
Odoo will help you adapt your website to fit your clients' needs perfectly. Easily integrate your internal policies, customize the contact form, showcase your latest products, and much more!

h1.strong: Do you want to go further?

template.p: Use the Accounting app to easily handle:

p.span: Use the Maintenance app to plan and monitor the upkeep of your production line.
Apply the recurring fee from the Subscription app to the maintenance agreement invoice
Use this app to manage your after-sales services

div.class: o_editor_banner lh-1 d-flex align-items-center alert alert-info pb-0 pt-3 o-contenteditable-false
w-100 ms-3 o_editor_banner_content o-contenteditable-true
div.p: Would you like to discuss your Odoo setup with us or go even further?
